assumptions: The valid_out signal is set to 1 when the data_out outputs 4 received data accumulation results. Otherwise, it is set to 0.
Task description was provided and only used as a low-risk planning hint.
Spec text was provided and only used where it did not contradict the contract.
LLM planning note: All added cases respect the unknown latency model; they only require observation of externally visible signals.
LLM planning note: No new ports or signals were introduced beyond those defined in the contract.

expected_properties: Observed outputs or protocol-visible signals settle to a stable, non-X post-reset state.
No fixed-cycle completion is assumed during reset recovery.
cases.goal: Establish a stable post-reset baseline before functional checking.
notes: Reset polarity may still be heuristic if the contract marks it ambiguous.
preconditions: Reset signal is available in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Assert the detected reset using the inferred polarity.
Release reset conservatively and observe interface stabilization.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

expected_properties: A legal operation causes an observable state or output change after conservative observation.
The plan does not assume a completion event before it is externally visible.
A legal operation (four consecutive valid_in=1 cycles) causes valid_out to assert for one cycle and data_out to present the sum of the four data_in values.
No output changes occur while valid_in is low.
cases.goal: Confirm correct accumulation and single‑cycle valid_out behavior under a single legal operation.
notes: Case intent is conservative when the contract is weak or timing is unresolved.
Conservative timing: do not assume exact latency between the fourth valid input and the valid_out pulse.
preconditions: Use only ports and observable signals present in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Drive a representative legal input pattern across known non-control inputs: ['data_in', 'valid_in']
Drive valid_in high for exactly four consecutive cycles with arbitrary data_in values.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.
After the fourth valid input, monitor outputs for at least two clock cycles to capture the valid_out pulse.

expected_properties: Boundary-value stimulation does not rely on hidden state or undocumented latency.
When width expressions are symbolic, only conservative min/max-style exploration is planned.
cases.goal: Exercise boundary-value and width-sensitive input patterns.
notes: Edge coverage remains value-oriented and avoids fixed-latency assumptions.
preconditions: At least one input or symbolic-width input is available.
stimulus_intent: Use zero-like, one-like, and boundary patterns on ['data_in', 'valid_in']
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

expected_properties: Repeated operations do not rely on hidden fixed-latency assumptions.
The second operation is observed only through externally visible acceptance, completion, or output change.
cases.goal: Observe repeated or back-to-back legal operations under conservative timing assumptions.
notes: When timing is unresolved, this case stays unresolved-safe and does not require deterministic overlap behavior.
preconditions: At least one legal operation is already defined by the basic case.
stimulus_intent: Apply two legal operations with minimal idle spacing that remains safe for the current contract strength.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

cases.defer_reason: Negative cases require stronger structured illegal-input semantics than the current contract provides.
expected_properties: When valid_in is 0, data_out and valid_out remain unchanged regardless of data_in changes.
cases.goal: Verify that the module ignores data_in when valid_in is deasserted.
notes: Ensures no accidental accumulation occurs on invalid inputs.
Negative cases require stronger structured illegal-input semantics than the current contract provides.
preconditions: Reset has been released and module is in stable post‑reset state.
stimulus_intent: Toggle data_in with random values while keeping valid_in low for several cycles.
timing_assumptions: Observe outputs for at least two clock cycles after each stimulus change.

expected_properties: valid_out is asserted for exactly one clock cycle after four consecutive valid inputs have been accumulated.
data_out reflects the sum of the four preceding valid data_in values.
cases.goal: Confirm correct handshaking of accumulation completion.
notes: Timing of the valid_out pulse is not assumed; only its single‑cycle width is checked.
preconditions: Module is out of reset and ready.
stimulus_intent: Drive valid_in high for four consecutive cycles with distinct data_in values, then deassert valid_in.
timing_assumptions: Allow an unknown number of cycles between the fourth valid input and the valid_out pulse, but verify the pulse lasts one cycle.

expected_properties: Two successive accumulations each produce a single-cycle valid_out and correct summed data_out.
No residual state from the first accumulation interferes with the second.
cases.goal: Validate correct behavior under consecutive accumulation cycles.
notes: Back‑to‑back operation is performed with minimal idle cycles while remaining conservative about latency.
preconditions: First accumulation has completed and valid_out returned to 0.
stimulus_intent: Perform two sets of four valid inputs back‑to‑back, each set with arbitrary data_in values.
timing_assumptions: Observe for at least two cycles after each set of four inputs to capture the valid_out pulse.

unresolved_items: Detected valid_ready-like signal(s) ['valid_in'] without matching role(s) ['ready'] in group 'in'.
Detected valid_ready-like signal(s) ['valid_out'] without matching role(s) ['ready'] in group 'out'.
